name: Step-3
archDisplay: MoE — 48 experts (3 routed + 1 shared per token), Multi-Matrix Factorization Attention (MFA) + Attention-FFN Disaggregation (AFD)
note: Open-source 321B MoE (38B active) multimodal reasoning VLM, Apache-2.0, co-designed with MFA/AFD for cost-effective decoding.
tpsNote: 321B total params must stay resident even with 38B active — infeasible on a single consumer GPU; needs multi-GPU/datacenter serving.

results.notes: AIME 2025. Also independently corroborated verbatim by SiliconFlow's launch coverage: https://www.siliconflow.com/blog/step3-now-live-on-siliconflow-the-leading-open-source-multimodal-reasoning-model
GPQA-Diamond. Corroborated verbatim by SiliconFlow's launch blog post.
LiveCodeBench (2024-08 to 2025-05 window). Corroborated verbatim by SiliconFlow's launch blog post.

MATH-Vision benchmark score for Step-3 (321B total/38B active). Read directly (not OCR'd) from the raw benchmark image figures/step3_bmk.jpeg, which is embedded identically in the GitHub README (github.com/stepfun-ai/Step3), the HF model card (huggingface.co/stepfun-ai/step3), and this official blog page. Downloading and visually inspecting the JPEG directly resolves the prior ambiguity noted in this model's verificationNotes: the full row reads MMMU 74.2, MATH-Vision 64.8, SimpleVQA 62.2, HallusionBench 64.2, ZeroBench(sub) 23.0, DynaMath 50.1, AIME25 82.9, GPQA-Diamond 73.0, LiveCodeBench(202408-202505) 67.1, HMMT25 70.0, CNMO24 83.7 — the existing mmmu/aime/gpqa/livecodebench entries all match this table exactly, confirming they are correct.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real and independently corroborated: 321B total/38B active MoE, Apache-2.0 code+weights, and July 31, 2025 release date all confirmed via HuggingFace (stepfun-ai/step3), GitHub (stepfun-ai/Step3), and the arXiv system paper (2507.19427, submitted 2025-07-25). Context length 64K (65536 tokens) confirmed via HF model card text. Pricing $0.57/$1.42 per 1M input/output tokens confirmed via independent pricing aggregator search. DROPPED 4 of 5 result rows (gpqa 67.1, aime 73, livecodebench 83.7, math-vision 64.2): the official capability-benchmark table on stepfun's research page exists only as an embedded image (figures/step3_bmk.jpeg per the GitHub README, not machine-readable text), and repeated independent fetch attempts of that same page/image produced mutually contradictory numbers for GPQA-Diamond (read as both 70.0 and 73.0), AIME25 (read as both 67.1 and 82.9), and LiveCodeBench (read as both 83.7 and 67.1) -- none could be literally confirmed with confidence, so per instructions they were dropped. Kept only MMMU=74.2, which was consistently and independently reproduced across three separate fetches/searches (direct page fetch, a third-party SiliconFlow blog post in narrative text, and general web search synthesis).

[2026-07-16] Checked all 5 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, swe-bench) for Step-3 (321B/38B, 2025-07-31) via all three required source types; found nothing citable for any of them -- concluding genuine non-publication, not omitted from copies. (1) Official materials: the launch page's benchmark image (chat.stepfun.com/research/en/step3, same JPEG embedded in the GitHub README and HF model card) was already visually confirmed pixel-by-pixel in a prior audit round to contain exactly 11 rows -- MMMU, MATH-Vision, SimpleVQA, HallusionBench, ZeroBench(sub), DynaMath, AIME25, GPQA-Diamond, LiveCodeBench, HMMT25, CNMO24 -- none of which are MMLU, HLE, MATH-500, HumanEval, or SWE-bench; this round's WebFetch of the GitHub README, HF model card, and both the arXiv abstract and full-text (2507.19427, ar5iv rendering) confirms the system paper itself contains zero capability-benchmark tables at all (it is a decoding-cost/system-co-design paper only, explicitly stating "In the future, we will release more details on the model side for Step-3"). (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/providers/stepfun) tracks only Step 3.5 Flash / Step 3.5 Flash 2603 / Step 3.7 Flash -- the original 321B Step-3 is entirely absent from their tracked model list, so no Intelligence-Index component scores exist for it. llm-stats.com/organizations/stepfun likewise lists only Step-3.5-Flash and Step3-VL-10B, no Step-3. Epoch AI's benchmarks.csv was already confirmed (prior sibling-model rounds) to contain zero StepFun rows of any kind. Scale AI's HLE leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard/humanitys_last_exam) has no StepFun/Step-3 entry. Vals.ai's models listing has no StepFun entry. A third-party aggregator (benchable.ai/models/stepfun-ai/step3) shows only vague, non-standard percentile categories ("Coding: 94.9%, 93rd percentile," "General Knowledge: 100%") that are not tied to any named standard benchmark and are not usable as citable humaneval/mmlu scores. (3) Sibling cross-reference: Step 3.7 Flash (2026-05-29) and Step 3.5 Flash are different-generation, differently-benchmarked models (their own tables cover swe-bench, swe-bench-pro, terminal-bench-2, browsecomp, hle, gdpval-aa -- not the same eval run as Step-3) and neither their results nor notes cite back to Step-3's own mmlu/hle/math/humaneval/swe-bench scores. Step-3's own technical report was previously confirmed (round covering sibling Step-1/Step-2) to compare only against DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen3-MoE-235B, with no retrospective StepFun-family comparison table. Conclusion: Step-3's public benchmark disclosure was deliberately narrow (vision-language + a handful of reasoning/math/code evals for its launch comparison table), and it appears MMLU, HLE, MATH-500, HumanEval, and SWE-bench were simply never run/published for this specific model -- consistent with the broader StepFun-family pattern already documented for Step-1 and Step-2 in this corpus.
